title: Flask app serving custom CSS from static folder

content.problemStatement:
In this worked example, you'll create a Flask application that serves custom CSS files from the default 'static' folder. You'll set up a route to display a simple HTML page that uses the custom CSS file.

### How does this work?
In `main.py`
1. Here we don't have to explicitly tell the Flask to look for static folder.
2. `return render_template('index.html')` renders the `index.html` file which should be inside the `templates/` folder.

In `index.html`
1. `` - This is standard HTML that tells the browser to load a CSS file for styling the page.
2. `{{ url_for('static', filename='custom.css') }}` - This is Jinja2 template syntax, used in Flask to generate URLs. \
`url_for('static', ...)`: Tells Flask to generate the correct URL for a static file. \
`filename='custom.css'`: Looks for custom.css inside your static folder.
